assertions.description: Letter is formatted as a formal business letter (Dear/Yours sincerely), 300-450 words, with production identified in the first paragraph
Specific practical details (crew size, dates, duration, equipment) are included or flagged as placeholders in brackets
At least one likely concern is addressed proactively (disruption, insurance, public access) and the letter closes with a specific proposed next step
Output is organized into standard categories (Camera, Lenses, Camera Support, Lighting, Audio, Monitoring, Power/Media, Grip, Expendables) with quantities specified for each item
Every item is appropriate for the stated production format and crew size — no contradictions (e.g., no heavy lighting rig for run-and-gun observational)
Output includes a Practical Notes section with at least one logistics tip specific to the shoot type, and expendables/commonly forgotten items are included
Output opens with a Research Overview section and contains numbered Archive Requests, each with Description, Editorial Function, Likely Sources, Availability Assessment, and Rights Note
Source recommendations are specific — named institutions or collections, not generic categories like 'news archives'
Availability assessments are honest — at least one request acknowledges footage may not exist or may be cost-prohibitive, and a Budget Tier Assessment is provided
Output is organized as a day-by-day schedule with time blocks formatted as a table (Start Time, Duration, Sequence, Notes) for each day
All scenes and subjects from the input are included — no omissions — and scenes are clustered by location to minimize travel
Each day includes a risk assessment and the schedule ends with a summary of shooting days, locations, and critical dependencies
Budget is structured with standard categories (Development, Pre-Production, Production, Post-Production, Overheads/Contingency) and each line item has unit basis, quantity, and rate range
A contingency line (typically 10%) is present, total range is clearly stated, and the currency matches the production region
Budget Notes section flags at least two specific cost risks relevant to this production type
Output contains all required sections: Header, Crew Calls (with individual times), Talent/Contributors, Location (with address and hospital), Schedule grid, Catering, Key Contacts, Notes
All times are in a consistent format throughout and every crew member has a specific call time
Any safety-critical missing information is flagged as 'CONFIRM BEFORE DISTRIBUTION' in bold, and schedule includes setup time and a meal break
Output contains all required sections: Subject Profile, Relevance to Film, On-Camera Assessment, Key Narrative Territory (3-5 areas), First Session Approach, and Sensitivities
Key Narrative Territory identifies specific, filmable areas (not general themes) with at least 3 distinct territories
Sensitivities section names a specific concern (not generic) and output includes a concrete Next Step note
Output contains all required sections: Location Function, Visual Requirements, Logistics and Access, Permissions and Clearances, Potential Problems and Contingencies
Visual requirements are specific — mentioning light direction, background elements, or minimum clear space, not generic ('needs to look good')
Contingency section anticipates at least two specific failure modes with practical alternatives for each
